_description: Open-ended format library. Formats are discoverable — this is a SEED, not a closed list. New formats added from reference analysis and persona behavioral data.

formats.name: Calculator Screen
formats.description: Phone calculator typing a price comparison in real-time. Math = undeniable proof.
formats.video_adaptation: Screen recording of calculation being typed: competitor price x days = monthly cost vs brand price x days = savings

formats.name: Search Bar Results
formats.description: Search engine with query = persona's internal monologue. Results = brand.
formats.video_adaptation: Screen recording: typing 'healthy lunch KL cheap', results loading, brand appears as top result

formats.name: iMessage / WhatsApp Chat
formats.description: Overheard conversation between friends recommending the product. Voyeuristic.
formats.video_adaptation: Text conversation typing in real-time with read receipts, blue/green bubbles

formats.name: Dating App Swipe
formats.description: Tinder-style left/right swipe. Left = competitor, Right = brand. Match confetti.
formats.video_adaptation: Swipe animation: left swipe on sad lunch (red X, price + cal), right swipe on brand (confetti, IT'S A MATCH)

formats.name: Phone Notification
formats.description: Push notification mockup creating urgency. Native mobile look.
formats.video_adaptation: Lock screen notification appears: deal alert, delivery update, or health milestone

formats.name: Health App / Fitness Tracker
formats.description: Health app dashboard showing improving stats. Data visualization of benefits.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated health dashboard: calories trending down, energy trending up, weight graph declining

formats.name: Shopping Cart / Checkout
formats.description: Shopee/Grab cart with items being added. Shopping behavior hijack.
formats.video_adaptation: Cart filling up with meals, running total showing savings vs competitor

formats.name: iPhone Notes Screenshot
formats.description: Private recommendation feel. Someone's personal list or review.
formats.video_adaptation: Screen recording of typing a meal plan / review list in Notes app

formats.name: Receipt / Bill
formats.description: Shopping receipt with line items = meals ordered. 'What she bought' voyeurism.
formats.video_adaptation: Receipt printing ASMR, items appearing one by one with prices and savings tags

formats.name: Fortune Cookie
formats.description: Physical fortune cookie crack + slip with brand-voice motivational text.
formats.video_adaptation: Cookie crack sound, slip pulled out, zoom on text with brand message

formats.name: Sticky Note / Post-It
formats.description: Casual handwritten note on desk or fridge. Personal, quick, intimate.
formats.video_adaptation: Post-it notes appearing on fridge/desk with meal plan, peeling off to reveal food underneath

formats.name: Menu Board / Chalkboard
formats.description: Restaurant-native format. Items + prices appearing on menu board.
formats.video_adaptation: Chalk-style text appearing on board with dish names and calories

formats.name: Weighing Scale Display
formats.description: Scale stepping on + number reveal. Transformation proof.
formats.video_adaptation: Feet stepping on scale, dramatic pause, number reveals with weekly progress

formats.name: Price Tag
formats.description: Retail price tag format. Crossed-out old price + new price. Value shock.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated price tags on meals: original delivery price crossed out, brand price highlighted

formats.name: Nutrition Label
formats.description: Health product label format showing cal, protein, fibre. Data proof.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated nutrition label with values filling in, compared to typical lunch

formats.name: Horoscope / Astrology Card
formats.description: Fortune reading format with star ratings per life area. Lunch fortune.
formats.video_adaptation: Mystical card appears, fortune categories flip one by one with star ratings, food reveal at end

formats.name: Bingo Card
formats.description: Shareable game — 'which ones apply to you?' Identity + engagement driver.
formats.video_adaptation: Bingo grid appearing with lunch habits, items getting checked off one by one

formats.name: Starter Pack
formats.description: Identity meme — 'the KL girl lunch starter pack.' Share trigger = confirmation.
formats.video_adaptation: Items appearing in grid format with labels, last item = brand product as the upgrade

formats.name: This or That
formats.description: Binary choice between two options. Engagement driver.
formats.video_adaptation: Split screen: left option (competitor) vs right option (brand), visual comparison

formats.name: Pros & Cons List
formats.description: Rational decision aid. Two columns appearing one by one.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated two-column list: cons of current lunch vs pros of brand

formats.name: Alignment Chart
formats.description: Personality sorting grid — 'which lunch type are you?' Share trigger = identity.
formats.video_adaptation: 3x3 grid filling in with lunch personality types, brand product in the 'best' quadrant

formats.name: Magazine Cover
formats.description: Aspirational, high fashion energy. Product elevated to editorial level.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated magazine cover with headlines appearing, food as cover star

formats.name: Polaroid Stack
formats.description: Nostalgic, authentic, collected memories. Week of meals as polaroids.
formats.video_adaptation: Polaroids falling/stacking with handwritten labels for each day's meal

formats.name: Split Screen Comparison
formats.description: Visual proof side-by-side. Before/after or competitor/brand.
formats.video_adaptation: Split screen: left = sad lunch reality, right = brand upgrade, both playing simultaneously

formats.name: Infographic
formats.description: Statistics and visual proof. Numbers make the case undeniable.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated data points appearing: RM saved, calories reduced, days of variety

formats.name: Price Comparison Table
formats.description: Rational decision aid. Row-by-row comparison filling in.
formats.video_adaptation: Table rows appearing: Grab vs brand vs home cook — price, time, calories, variety columns

formats.name: Progress Chart / Graph
formats.description: Transformation over time. Graph line moving = visual proof of improvement.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated line chart: weight/energy/spending over 4 weeks of brand meals

formats.name: Star Rating / Review Card
formats.description: Social proof in familiar review format. Stars filling in with category scores.
formats.video_adaptation: Review card with stars animating: taste 4.5/5, value 5/5, health 5/5, variety 4.8/5

formats.name: Countdown Timer
formats.description: Urgency and anticipation. Timer counting down to deal or reveal.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated countdown: deal expires, limited slots, seasonal menu drop

formats.name: Pure UGC Testimonial
formats.description: No format hijack — authentic KOL talking to camera. The baseline.
formats.video_adaptation: Direct: KOL face hook → story → food reveal → CTA. Classic assembled pipeline.

formats.name: Compiled Montage
formats.description: Multiple KOL clips + VO narration. Hybrid audio (A+I=KOL, D+Act=VO).
formats.video_adaptation: Fast-cut montage: KOL clips for social proof, VO for narrative, food B-roll for proof

formats.name: VO Montage
formats.description: Multiple KOL clips with full voiceover narration over ALL blocks. KOL audio muted.
formats.video_adaptation: Fast-cut montage: KOL faces for authenticity, continuous VO narration for message control

formats.name: What I Eat in a Day/Week
formats.description: Food diary format. Multiple dishes shown in sequence with day labels.
formats.video_adaptation: Day 1 → Day 2 → ... format with dish close-ups, prices, calories per day

formats.name: Before / After Transformation
formats.description: Result-first reveal → explain how. Proof then story.
formats.video_adaptation: Show the transformation first, then rewind to explain the journey with product

formats.name: Problem-Agitate-Solve
formats.description: Classic PAS: state the pain, make it worse, present the solution.
formats.video_adaptation: Pain point scenario → agitation → product reveal as solution

formats.name: Calendar/Planner View
formats.description: Weekly or monthly calendar with meal slots filled in. Progress tracking meets meal planning.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated calendar filling in day-by-day with meals, checkmarks appearing, before/after week comparison

formats.name: WhatsApp Group Chat
formats.description: Fake WhatsApp group conversation about ordering food. Deeply familiar UI for Malaysian audience.
formats.video_adaptation: Animated chat messages appearing in group: 'eh lunch order what?', 'I saw this Mirra thing', reactions, voice notes

formats.name: Aesthetic Flat Lay
formats.description: Top-down desk/table arrangement — the Instagram/XHS content creation format. Aspirational lifestyle framing.
formats.video_adaptation: Stop-motion style flat lay being arranged: meal, drink, laptop, phone, accessories. Perfect for WFH audience.

formats.name: Album Cover / Playlist Card
formats.description: Spotify/Apple Music album art style with food as cover image. Gen-Z music culture meets food.
formats.video_adaptation: Album cover reveal: title card with meal photo, track list = meal components, artist = brand

formats.name: Caught Red-Handed Skit
formats.description: Drama skit where someone is 'caught' with product — product is the punchline, not the pitch. Mini-drama energy.
formats.video_adaptation: 24mm handheld, chaotic energy, phone-quality: someone sneaking food → dramatic discovery → product reveal as punchline

formats.name: Office Jealousy Documentary Skit
formats.description: Colleagues jealous of one person's meal. Documentary-style comedy with exaggerated staring and zoom-ins.
formats.video_adaptation: 35mm documentary zoom on each jealous face, nature-documentary-meets-office-comedy narration energy, meal reveal as climax

formats.name: Confession Mirror
formats.description: Bathroom mirror confession setup — viewer expects drama, twist is about food not relationship. Mood flip IS the hook.
formats.video_adaptation: Bathroom mirror selfie angle, dim emotional lighting → warm bright reveal. Two-tone lighting: vulnerable setup → product reveal

formats.name: Expectation vs Reality Split
formats.description: Meme split-screen: exaggerated sad diet food vs product reality at same calories. The contrast IS the argument.
formats.video_adaptation: Two parallel food shots with EXTREME contrast — left: cold, desaturated funeral-mood sad diet | right: warm, golden, steaming restaurant-quality Mirra meal

formats.name: Blindfold Taste Test (反转)
formats.description: Blindfolded eating with shock reveal — 抖音 15-second reversal formula. Conflict (0-5s) → fast cuts (5-10s) → reversal (10s).
formats.video_adaptation: Two-camera feel, clean kitchen, blindfolded tasting → chewing close-ups → zoom punch on reaction face at reveal moment

formats.name: Steam Backlight Reveal
formats.description: Steaming dish backlit against dark bg. Low angle through rising steam. Golden warm light. The #1 food money shot.
formats.video_adaptation: Sora 4s: low angle macro, backlit warm golden light, steam fills upper frame, food sharp foreground, 50mm shallow DOF, dark background

formats.name: Sauce Pour / Drizzle
formats.description: Viscous liquid slow-motion pour — honey, curry, sambal, gravy. Continuous stream catches backlight. Never cut away.
formats.video_adaptation: Sora 4s: overhead slow-mo pour, viscous stream catches warm backlight, sauce pools and spreads, 85mm macro extreme shallow DOF

formats.name: First-Bite Crunch (ASMR)
formats.description: Extreme close-up breaking/biting crispy food. Sound = 50% of hook. No BGM — food sounds ARE the audio. Neurological appetite trigger.
formats.video_adaptation: Sora 4s: macro hands breaking crispy food, steaming interior revealed, crumbs scatter, 100mm lens, warm side light, ASMR crunch sound

formats.name: Sizzle Drop
formats.description: Food hitting hot pan — instant sizzle, oil spray backlit, steam burst. Camera at pan level shooting UP. ASMR dopamine trigger.
formats.video_adaptation: Sora 4s: low-angle at pan level, food drops onto hot oiled surface, sizzle + oil micro-spray backlit, camera push-in, 35mm, high contrast warm

formats.name: Slice / Cut Reveal
formats.description: Sharp knife cutting through dish revealing colorful interior cross-section. Hidden layers trigger curiosity completion. 60fps slow-mo.
formats.video_adaptation: Sora 4s: close-up knife slicing through dish, colorful layered interior revealed, steam escapes, 45° slow push-in, 50mm macro

formats.name: Overhead Plating Assembly
formats.description: Top-down view, ingredients placed one by one. Each addition adds color/texture. The PROCESS is the hook — oddly satisfying assembly.
formats.video_adaptation: Sora 8s: overhead static, clean bowl on dark surface, hands place rice → vegetables → protein → sauce spiral, each ingredient adds visual pop

formats.name: Unboxing / Lid Lift Reveal
formats.description: Hands lifting lid off meal container — condensation catches light, steam escapes, colorful food revealed. Product motion start + curiosity.
formats.video_adaptation: Sora 4s: overhead, hands lift branded lid, condensation on lid, steam escape, slow push-in, 50mm shallow DOF, warm lifestyle tones

hook_types.pattern_interrupt: Format LOOKS WRONG for a food ad — brain must resolve the incongruity
hook_types.curiosity_gap: Open loop that demands closing — 'what happens after...', 'what's inside...'
hook_types.identity_confirmation: Viewer sees themselves — 'this is SO me', 'confirm us la'
hook_types.fomo: Others have it, you don't — 'everyone's ordering this'
hook_types.value_shock: Price or benefit breaks expectations — 'RM19 for all this?!'
hook_types.recognition: Mirrors unconscious daily behavior — 'I literally do this every day'
hook_types.voyeurism: Peeking into private life — chat, receipt, order, notes

share_trigger_types.identity: Sharing = declaring identity. 'This is literally me.' Bingo, starter pack, alignment chart.
share_trigger_types.discovery: Sharing = being the person who finds things first. 'Wait have you seen this?!' Price reveal, new menu.
share_trigger_types.group_identity: Sharing = strengthening social bonds. 'Tag your lunch kaki.' Group order, office lunch.
share_trigger_types.utility: Sharing = providing value to someone specific. 'Forward this to someone who needs cheap healthy lunch.'
share_trigger_types.reaction: Sharing = emotional bonding over shared experience. 'Too real.' Relatable pain, meme.

format_categories.ui_mimicry: Digital interfaces the persona interacts with daily. Highest pattern interrupt potential.
format_categories.physical_object: Tangible objects from daily life. Tactile, familiar, grounded.
format_categories.cultural_lifestyle: Social media native formats. Identity and sharing driven.
format_categories.native_video: No format hijack — pure video content. The baseline for assembled pipeline.
format_categories.food_sensory: Sensory-first food hooks — the food IS the scroll-stop. Steam, sauce, crunch, sizzle, slice, plating. Best for Desire phase and standalone food content.
